output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? A Tenant’s Urgent Recovery Guide
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, state deadlines, and how to write a demand letter that gets your money back fast.
content_outline: Introduction: When Your Security Deposit Is Not Returned
Can My Landlord Keep My Security Deposit? Know the Law
What Can a Landlord Legally Deduct (and What They Can’t)
How Long Does a Landlord Have to Return Your Deposit by State?
My Landlord Won’t Give Me My Deposit Back: Step-by-Step Response
Security Deposit Dispute Letter: Templates and Timing
Escalation Tactics: From Demand Letter to Small Claims Court
Final Checklist: Get Your Deposit Back Before Time Runs Out
key_points: Most states require landlords to return deposits within 14–30 days with an itemized statement; missing the deadline weakens their position
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear, pre-existing damage, or vague 'cleaning' fees without proof
A professionally written security deposit demand letter is the fastest way to force compliance before filing suit
Tenants should document everything—lease terms, photos, move-out receipts, and written communications—to build a winning case
If a landlord refuses to return a deposit after a formal demand, small claims court is the next step—and many states allow penalty damages
output.cta: Don’t let your landlord keep what’s yours. Create your state-specific security deposit demand letter with DepositBack in minutes and start your recovery today.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware US renters whose landlord is withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and need urgent help recovering their money
